name: A Lucky Accident
recipeInstructions:
1. Place 3 quarts of water in the bottom of a large stock pot with a pasta insert.
2. Add the tomato liquid and tomato paste to the pot.
3. Put all of the other ingredients (carrots, parsnips, turnip, celery, onion, parsley, bay leaf, rosemary, thyme, black peppercorns) in the pasta insert.
4. Bring to a boil over high heat.
5. Lower the heat and cover. Simmer for about 3 hours.
6. Lift out the pasta insert, allowing any liquid to fall back into the pot.
7. Press down on the solids with a spoon or spatula to extract as much liquid as possible.
8. Return all the extracted liquid to the pot.
9. Discard the solids.
Note: This started out as a vegetable stock recipe to use up some leftover ingredients, but after straining the stock, I decided to use it as a cooking liquid for pasta. The pasta thickened and flavored the stock so much that I ended up using the leftovers to make a tomato-vegetable soup. I may add more vegetables and small pasta to the soup later.
